title: Home
text:
Start here




Projects →
Short case studies showing what you did, how you did it, and why it matters.




Résumé for Research →
A narrative CV using four modules, aligned with current funder expectations.




Outputs →
Publications, talks, software, datasets, and other things you’ve put into the world.

title: Résumé for Research
text:
Note



This format focuses on contributions and impact, rather than job titles or publication counts. Wherever possible, link to evidence that supports your claims.
This Résumé for Research uses a narrative CV structure, commonly requested by research funders and institutions.
Contributions are grouped into four modules. For each entry, aim to make clear:

title: Résumé for Research
section: 1. Contributions to the generation of knowledge
text:
1. Contributions to the generation of knowledge




Contribution title or short claim

Contribution: What you did or produced.
Significance: Why this advances understanding in your field.
Evidence: Link to a paper, preprint, repository, report, or dataset.

title: Résumé for Research
section: 2. Contributions to the development of others
text:
2. Contributions to the development of others




Contribution title or short claim

Contribution: e.g. supervision, mentoring, teaching, onboarding resources.
Who benefited: students, colleagues, teams, or communities.
Evidence: materials, projects, acknowledgements, feedback.

title: Résumé for Research
section: 3. Contributions to the wider research community
text:
3. Contributions to the wider research community




Contribution title or short claim

Contribution: What you shared, led, or enabled.
Reach: Who uses it, or how widely it has been adopted.
Evidence: repositories, usage, citations, invitations.

title: Résumé for Research
text:
4. Contributions to broader society




Contribution title or short claim

Contribution: Activity, output, or engagement.
Impact: Why this matters beyond academia.
Evidence: talks, collaborations, media, outputs.
